title: $occvalidation
text: $occvalidation Check whether an occurrence requires validation. $occvalidation { ( Entity ) } Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Entity String Entity name. If omitted, the current entity is used. Return Values Table 2. Values returned by $occvalidation Value Meaning "" Entity is not valid, for example, the field name is not drawn. 0 Occurrence does not require validation, either because the occurrence has not been modified or it has already been successfully modified. Check the value of $occmod to determine which of these situations applies. 1 Occurrence requires validation. Use Allowed in all component types, except self-contained Reports. Description $occvalidation enables you to check whether an occurrence requires validation. An occurrence needs validation in either of the following circumstances: Data in one of the fields of the occurrence has been modified ( $occmod is 1), but has not yet been successfully validated ( $occvalidation is also 1). Validation has been demanded by ProcScript ( $occcheck is 1), regardless of the value of $occvalidation . Example: Using $occvalidation In this example $occvalidation returns the validation status of the current occurrence of an entity. ; DUMMY and FLD2 are non-database fields ; enter an appropriate entity name in FLD2 vEntity = $occvalidation("%%FLD2%%%") selectcase(vEntity) case "" message/error "Entity name is not valid" case 0 message/info "The occurrence is valid" case 1 message/info "The occurrence requires validation" endselectcase Copy Related Links Validate Determines whether the valerr data processing attribute is added for the selected entity node in the DTD tree. This attribute holds the occurrence or field validation error (depending on whether the selected item is an entity or field). Valid values are True or False (default). validateocc Validate an occurrence. $fieldvalidation Identify whether a field requires validation. $instancevalidation Identify whether data in the component instance requires validation. $keyvalidation Identify whether a primary or candidate key requires validation. $occcheck Return or set the requirement for checking an occurrence. $occmod Return the modification status of an occurrence.
